body:
Weekly status

Counseling office notes, week of 2026-10-05. Henrico Fields Middle School.

  • Wins: Completed check-ins with all 6th-grade homerooms following back-to-school adjustment period. Three students flagged for follow-up responded well to initial sessions.
  • Concerns: Two referrals from Ms. Tran (7th grade science) for behavioral concerns — scheduling individual sessions this week. One student on IEP showing increased anxiety ahead of state testing window.
  • Follow-ups: Confirm parent meeting with the Morrison family (Owen's classmate situation), send resource list to Dr. Fredericks for the SEL committee, touch base with school psychologist re: 504 review for student #1142.
title: Weekly status

body:
Quarterly counseling plan — Q1 SY 2026-27

Planning document for school counseling priorities, October 2026 through December 2026.

Goals this quarter

  • Complete individual check-ins for all students on the at-risk roster (approx. 38 students)
  • Launch Lunch Bunch social skills group for 6th graders — target start date week of 2026-10-12
  • Coordinate with special education team on IEP counseling goals for 14 students
  • Deliver two classroom guidance lessons per grade level on study skills and growth mindset

Calendar anchors

  • October 14: School Counseling Awareness Month kickoff display in main hallway
  • October 22: College & Career Night (co-facilitating with admin)
  • November 5: PTA meeting — counseling update to parents
  • December 12: End-of-semester check-in reports to principal

Notes

Budget for small-group materials submitted to front office. Awaiting approval from Dr. Fredericks.
title: Quarterly counseling plan

body:
PTA meeting notes — March 2026

PTA meeting held 2026-03-24 at Henrico Fields Middle School, 6:30 PM. Attendees: approx. 22 parents, Principal Fredericks, Kelly West (counselor), two 8th-grade teachers.

Counseling update shared with parents

  • Reviewed school counseling services available to students: individual sessions, small groups, classroom guidance, crisis support
  • Reminded parents of the anonymous concern form on the school website for reporting peer issues
  • Highlighted upcoming 8th-grade transition night scheduled for Thursday, October 16 — pending final auditorium confirmation

Parent questions

  • Several parents asked about the stress and anxiety trend they are seeing at home — agreed to share a resource handout via the school newsletter
  • One parent raised bullying concern on the bus route; referred to Dr. Fredericks and the dean of students for follow-up

Action items

  • Kelly: Draft parent resource sheet on teen stress — send to PTA president by 2026-10-01
  • Dr. Fredericks: Confirm 8th-grade transition night date and reserve auditorium
  • PTA: Add counseling office hours to the monthly newsletter going forward
title: PTA meeting notes

body:
Student support follow-up — March 2026

Follow-up notes for active cases as of 2026-03-24. Confidential — counseling office use only.

Open cases

  • Student A (7th grade): Second session completed. Showing improvement in peer conflict management. Parents notified of progress. Third session scheduled for 2026-03-31.
  • Student B (6th grade): Referred by homeroom teacher for social withdrawal. Initial session held 2026-03-22. Student was receptive. Checking in again next week.
  • Student C (8th grade): Ongoing anxiety management support — biweekly sessions. Working on high school transition stress. Parents involved and supportive.

Closed this period

  • Student D (7th grade): Peer mediation completed successfully. Both students and teachers report improved dynamic. Case closed 2026-03-20.

Referral pipeline

Two new referrals received from guidance team this week — intake appointments to be scheduled by end of March.
title: Student support follow-up

body:
New student onboarding checklist

Checklist for welcoming and integrating new students to Henrico Fields Middle School. Updated 2026-10-02.

Day 1 — Counselor tasks

  • Pull schedule and confirm no conflicts or missing elective placements
  • Brief welcome meeting with student (10–15 min) — tour of counseling office, introduce services, answer questions
  • Connect student with assigned peer ambassador if available

Week 1

  • Check in with homeroom teacher on adjustment
  • Confirm IEP or 504 records requested from prior school if applicable
  • Add student to caseload tracking sheet

Week 2–3

  • Follow-up check-in with student — how are lunches going, any social concerns
  • Notify parent/guardian of counseling contact info and availability
  • Flag for small group placement if social integration support needed

Notes

Three new enrollments this fall — all at 6th grade level. Two transferred mid-year from out of state.
title: New student onboarding checklist

body:
Field trip planning — Richmond Science Museum, November 2026

Coordination notes for the 7th-grade field trip to the Science Museum of Virginia. Counseling office involvement: supporting students with sensory or anxiety needs. Page created 2026-10-04.

Logistics

  • Date: 2026-11-07 (Friday), 8:00 AM – 2:30 PM
  • Transportation: Two school buses, confirmed by front office
  • Chaperones needed: 6 (teachers filling 4 spots, 2 parent volunteers still needed)

Counseling considerations

  • Two students on 504 plans — reviewed accommodations with lead teacher Ms. Rivera
  • One student with documented sensory sensitivity — confirmed quiet room availability at museum
  • Reminder to all chaperones: no social media photos of students

Pre-trip

  • Parent permission slips due 2026-10-28
  • Brief classroom check-in week of 2026-10-19 to address any student anxiety about the trip
title: Field trip planning

body:
Counseling supplies budget — SY 2026-27

Budget tracking for school counseling office materials and small-group supplies. Fiscal year starts July 2026. Page updated 2026-10-11.

Approved budget

  • Total allocation: $650 for the school year
  • Source: Building discretionary fund, confirmed by Dr. Fredericks in August

Spending to date

  • $84.50 — feelings charts, dry-erase pockets, fidget tools (ordered October 2026)
  • $31.20 — printer ink cartridge for counseling office printer
  • $22.00 — copy paper (1 ream, shared with main office)
  • Total spent: $137.70

Remaining: $512.30

Planned purchases

  • Social skills card games for Lunch Bunch group — est. $45
  • Calm-down corner refresh (new stress balls, visual timer) — est. $60
  • College & Career Night materials (folders, stickers, handouts) — est. $80
title: Counseling supplies budget

body:
Family engagement one-pager — Spring 2026

Draft communication for parents explaining counseling services and how to request support. Created 2026-03-19 for distribution at spring parent night and via school newsletter.

What school counseling provides

  • Individual check-in meetings (request through teacher or call the counseling office directly)
  • Small-group support for social skills, friendship, and stress management
  • Classroom guidance lessons each quarter on topics like study habits, empathy, and high school planning
  • Crisis support and referrals to community resources when needed

How to reach us

  • Call the main office and ask for the counseling office
  • Submit an anonymous concern using the link on the school website
  • Email via the school directory (responses within 1 school day)

A note from Kelly

The counseling office is a place where every student is welcome. You do not need to be in crisis to visit. If your child is adjusting, struggling socially, or just needs a quiet check-in, please reach out. We are here.
title: Family engagement one-pager
